221 , 240)"><font style="font-size: 15px">In ReactiveX an observer subscribes to an Observable. Observable follows the observer design pattern. Then that observer reacts to whatever item or sequence of items the Observable emits. This pattern facilitates concurrent operations because it does not need to block while waiting for the Observable to emit objects, but instead, it creates a sentry in the form of an observer that stands ready to react appropriately at whatever future time the Observable does so.</font></span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="100" width="800" x="20" y="360" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell #608b4e">//&nbsp;next&nbsp;pushes&nbsp;the&nbsp;next&nbsp;data&nbsp;package.</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;Emitting&nbsp;string&nbsp;data&nbsp;types</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;setTimeout<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(()</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">=&gt;</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">{</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;observer<span style="color: #dcdcdc">.</span>next<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(</span><span style="color: #ce9178">'FIRST&nbsp;RESPONSE****:'</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">)</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">},</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #b5cea8">2000</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">);</span></div><br><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;Emit&nbsp;the&nbsp;second&nbsp;data&nbsp;package&nbsp;after&nbsp;4&nbsp;seconds.</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;next&nbsp;pushes&nbsp;the&nbsp;next&nbsp;data&nbsp;package.</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;Emitting&nbsp;string&nbsp;data&nbsp;types</span></div><br><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;setTimeout<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(()</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">=&gt;</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">{</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;observer<span style="color: #dcdcdc">.</span>next<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(</span><span style="color: #ce9178">'SECOND&nbsp;RESPONSE****:'</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">)</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">},</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #b5cea8">4000</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">);</span></div><br><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;Emit&nbsp;the&nbsp;error&nbsp;package&nbsp;after&nbsp;5&nbsp;seconds.</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;error&nbsp;emits&nbsp;the&nbsp;error&nbsp;response.</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;Emitting&nbsp;string&nbsp;data&nbsp;types</span></div><br><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;setTimeout<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(()</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">=&gt;</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">{</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;observer<span style="color: #dcdcdc">.</span>error<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(</span><span style="color: #ce9178">'ERROR&nbsp;RESPONSE****:'</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">)</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">},</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #b5cea8">5000</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">)</span></div><br><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;Emits&nbsp;the&nbsp;complete.</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;setTimeout<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(()</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">=&gt;</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">{</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;observer<span style="color: #dcdcdc">.</span>complete<span style="color: #dcdcdc">();</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">},</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #b5cea8">6000</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">)</span></div><br><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">});</span></div><br><div>&nbsp;</div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;Subsribe&nbsp;the&nbsp;observable</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;first&nbsp;callbacks&nbsp;take&nbsp;success.</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;second&nbsp;callbacks&nbsp;takes&nbsp;failure</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #608b4e">//&nbsp;third&nbsp;call&nbsp;backs&nbsp;takes&nbsp;completion.</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #569cd6">const</span>&nbsp;customSubscrition&nbsp;<span #dcdcdc">{</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;console<span style="color: #dcdcdc">.</span>log<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">},</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(</span>error<span style="color: #dcdcdc">:</span>&nbsp;<span style="color: #569cd6">string</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">);</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">},</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: style="color: #dcdcdc">.</span>log<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(</span><span style="color: #ce9178">"COMPLETION:"</span><span style="color: #dcdcdc">);</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">}</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">);</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color: #dcdcdc">}</span></div><br><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;ngOnDestroy<span style="color: #dcdcdc">(){</span></div><div>&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;<span span.font: In ReactiveX an observer subscribes to an Observable. Observable follows the observer design pattern. Then that observer reacts to whatever item or sequence of items the Observable emits. This pattern facilitates concurrent operations because it does not need to block while waiting for the Observable to emit objects, but instead, it creates a sentry in the form of an observer that stands ready to react appropriately at whatever future time the Observable does so.

p.span:
Interval create an Observable that
emits a sequence of integers spaced by a given time interval

span.span:
is an instance method of Observable as well as a
standalone RxJS function. ... pipe accepts operators as arguments such as filter , map , mergeScan etc with comma separated and execute them in a sequence they are passed in as arguments and finally returns Observable instance.

div.span: // Create method takes function as a argument with Observer.
// Emit the first data package after 2 seconds.
// next pushes the next data package.
// Emit the second data package after 4 seconds.
// next pushes the next data package.
// Emit the error package after 5 seconds.
// This will not be called since there is an error at 5 seconds before the completion.
